question: What governance properties must a multi-agent orchestration framework satisfy to prevent unbounded reasoning loops while preserving legitimate iterative problem-solving?
hypothesis: Termination must be enforced externally because the same reasoning that generates a loop can generate a false convergence signal
test_method: formal_proof + thought_experiment (halting problem analogy + architectural review)
evidence: Turing halting problem: no algorithm can decide termination for all programs
Demerzel autonomous-loop-policy: 12-cycle cap, progress test, kill switch
Seldon Plan: 6/day cap + novelty registry as measurable progress indicator
Ralph Loop pattern: external convergence metric (test pass rate) as termination criterion

cross_model_agreement.claude_verdict: confirmed — halting problem grounds external termination requirement
